fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer audiologist license to new hampshire, audiologist compact new hampshire, new hampshire audiologist endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)
board.mailing_address: Office of Professional Licensure and Certification, 7 Eagle Square, Concord, NH 03301

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Register for a QuickSTART Account
steps.description: Navigate to the QuickSTART online licensing system, click 'Register' at the top right, and follow the prompts to create your applicant account.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into your QuickSTART account, select 'Apply for a License', choose 'Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists', and complete the Audiologist application form, providing all requested personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload official transcripts from all academic institutions, proof of supervised clinical experience (e.g., Clinical Fellowship Year or equivalent), and official score reports for the national examination (Praxis) directly through QuickSTART or have them sent to the OPLC.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: All applicants must submit to a criminal background check (CBC) as required by the OPLC. Follow the instructions provided by OPLC regarding the CBC process, which typically involves fingerprinting and submitting associated forms.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100.00 via credit card through the QuickSTART system at the time of application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log into your QuickSTART account to check the status of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Board or OPLC staff.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. New Hampshire participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Hampshire without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in New Hampshire, submit your application and all required documentation through the New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification's (OPLC) QuickSTART online licensing system.
